chapter_title.ar: بَابُ اسْتِوَاءِ الْعَمَلِ وَ الْمُدَاوَمَةِ عَلَيْهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 41 – Regularity of the deed and the persistence upon it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (82/ 8)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (82/ 8)
text: كَانَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ ( صلوات الله عليه )  يَقُولُ إِنِّي لَأُحِبُّ أَنْ أَقْدِمَ عَلَى رَبِّي وَ عَمَلِي مُسْتَوٍ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Al A’ala, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> was saying: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> love to go forward to my<sup>asws</sup> Lord<sup>azwj</sup> and my<sup>asws</sup> deed is regular’.
en.sarwar: 5. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Fadalah ibn Ayyub from al-‘Ala’ from Muhammad ibn Muslim from abu la’ far (a.s.) who has said the following: “Ali ibn al-Husayn (a.s.) would say, ‘I love to go before my Lord with my deeds orderly and balanced.’”
